business.headline: Can motor insurers reject claims for reporting accidents late?Handcrafted ByOur Editors In the fifth story in our series Motor Mess, we spoke to experts to understand why delayed accident reporting often leads to insurance claim rejections, the mistakes policyholders should avoid and what options remain if such claims are rejected.

Two inspections, one pattern: Labour off the booksIn the sixth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, multiple factory inspections conducted in 1988 expose recurring patterns of undocumented labour, hidden wage records and statutory evasion.

Petrol and diesel prices likely to be hiked before May 15Petrol and diesel prices in India are likely to be hiked before May 15 as state-run oil marketing companies face rising financial stress amid a sharp surge in global crude oil prices, sources told India Today TV.

Bringing mothers in as co-applicants: Redefining home loan ownership in IndiaFamilies are now increasingly choosing to involve mothers in building long-term financial assets and securing a stronger financial future for themselves.

From flowers to financial freedom: Rethinking Mother's DayFor years, Mother's Day has been about flowers and celebrations. But slowly, conversations are shifting towards something deeper, i.e., helping mothers build savings, investments, and financial confidence of their own.

Explained: Why SBI shares tumbled nearly 7% after Q4 resultsSBI shares fell sharply after the lender's Q4 earnings disappointed the market. The reaction underscored investor concern over treasury income, margins and earnings quality.

Sensex closes over 500 points lower as SBI tumbles nearly 7% after Q4 resultsBenchmark indices closed sharply lower on Thursday after SBI's weaker-than-expected Q4 results hit banking shares. The decline reflected caution around crude oil prices, West Asia tensions and the broader earnings season.

Sensex sinks 600 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Benchmark indices fell sharply on Thursday as higher crude prices, foreign selling and weak banking stocks hit sentiment. Investors stayed cautious ahead of key Q4 results, with traders watching US-Iran tensions and oil moves.

I broke my FD 3 times: When it helped and when it hurtA fixed deposit feels safe because it is supposed to sit quietly in the background, growing slowly over time. But when life throws sudden surprises, that safety net often becomes the first thing people reach for. The real question is, when does breaking an FD actually make sense, and when does it backfire?

Think PAN is needed only for ITR Filing? 7 transactions where you must quote itPAN has quietly become one of the most important financial documents in India today, but are you aware that many banking, investment and cash transactions cannot be completed without quoting it?

8th Pay Commission: The family unit formula behind salary revisions of govt employeesEmployee unions have urged the 8th Pay Commission to revisit the family unit formula used to fix salaries. Any revision could raise basic pay, allowances, pensions and the broader compensation bill. But what exactly is the 'family unit' formula?

Tata Trusts board meeting postponed amid Tata Sons governance, listing discussionsTata Trusts has postponed a crucial board meeting scheduled for today to May 16, as governance disputes, Tata Sons board representation, listing discussions and leadership questions continue to draw attention across corporate India.

Lenskart shares fall over 2% amid block deal buzz, IPO lock-in expiryLenskart shares fell in Thursday's trade as block deal chatter coincided with the expiry of its IPO lock-in period. The move put the spotlight on possible supply pressure even as analysts said fundamentals remain unchanged.

Sensex opens 400 points lower, Nifty slips below 24,200 as banking stocks dragThe BSE Sensex fell 422.11 points, or 0.54%, to 77,422.41 in early trade. The NSE Nifty50 slipped 129 points to 24,197.65, falling below the 24,200 mark.

Why is Maharashtra's Rs 100 Will registration move getting so much attention?Anyone who owns assets such as a flat, land, jewellery, fixed deposits, shares or mutual funds should consider making a Will. Even small savings can become the reason for family disagreements if there is no clear-written instruction from the owner.

Pronto bags fresh $20 million funding, valuation jumps to $200 millionPronto offers quick home services such as laundry, kitchen preparation and home cleaning, with services starting from Rs 125. Workers are available within 15 minutes through the platform.

Stock market ends flat as Sensex slips 114, Nifty stays above 24,300Benchmark equity indices ended little changed on Wednesday, with the Sensex slipping 114 points and the Nifty holding above 24,300. The muted session underlined investor caution over global cues, interest rate expectations and earnings momentum, while selective buying capped losses.

Can senior citizens above skip ITR filing in FY27? Here's who qualifiesFiling income tax returns every year can be stressful for many elderly taxpayers. But did you know some senior citizens above 75 may not need to file ITR in FY27 at all if certain conditions are met?

Bought a car above Rs 10 lakh? You may be missing this tax creditCar buyers paying over Rs 10 lakh are being reminded to check the 1% TCS collected at purchase. The credit, linked to PAN records, can reduce tax dues or increase refunds at ITR filing.

Ex-CJI DY Chandrachud to mediate Sunjay Kapur family feud between mother, widowThe Supreme Court has pushed for a settlement in the Sunjay Kapur family trust dispute, with former CJI DY Chandrachud stepping in as mediator after both sides agreed to explore resolution through mediation.

entertainment.headline: Vijay Deverakonda stuns in Rowdy Janardhana poster as Keerthy Suresh takes charge The makers of Rowdy Janardhana unveiled a birthday poster showing Vijay Deverakonda and Keerthy Suresh in a striking face-off. The poster teases the film's mix of rural action and romance ahead of its December 2026 release.

Nora Fatehi to perform at FIFA World Cup 2026 opening ceremony in Canada Nora Fatehi is set to perform at the FIFA World Cup 2026 opening ceremony in Canada on June 12. The announcement marks her return to the FIFA stage as the tournament opens across three host countries.

Vijay Deverakonda waves at fans, greets them with Rashmika on 37th birthday Fans gathered outside actor Vijay Deverakonda's home for his birthday celebrations on Saturday. The actor waved at the crowd, cut a cake and was joined by his wife, actor Rashmika Mandanna.

Main Vaapas Aaunga: Sharvari's note for Imtiaz Ali hints at their bond on set Actor Sharvari shared behind-the-scenes moments of the song Maskara from her upcoming film Main Ghar Aaunga. She also posted an emotional note for director Imtiaz Ali, thanking him for helping her discover new shades in her performance.

Will Diljit Dosanjh become Punjab's political face? Singer sets record straight Actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh reacted to calls asking him to enter Punjab politics. In an X post, he made it clear that he wants to stay focused on entertainment.

Will The Devil Wears Prada get part 3? Director David Frankel reacts to buzz Director David Frankel said he would be open to returning for a third The Devil Wears Prada film. The cast, including Meryl Streep and Anne Hathaway, earlier shared what would be needed for part three to happen.

This is not us: Karan-Tejasswi fight it out in flashy outfits in Desi Bling promo The new promo of Desi Bling shows a tense moment between actors Karan Kundrra and Tejasswi Prakash after she tells him he gets influenced by people around him. The clip ends with emotional scenes, tears and a conversation about their relationship.

Stop PR games: Ameesha Patel questions actors 'buying' superstar status Actor Ameesha Patel slammed Bollywood's PR games and said actors should call themselves a superstar only after creating history and havoc at the box office. She also said her PR machinery is weak, unlike others, while talking about her blockbuster films.

Win for Tamil Nadu: Genelia D'Souza calls Vijay's election victory proud moment Actor Genelia D'Souza said Thalapathy Vijay's election victory is a proud moment for Tamil Nadu. She said the result reflected public trust in him and faith that he would meet expectations.

Is Yash's global dream proving toxic to his pan-India superstardom? What began as anticipation has turned into a test of patience for fans. Yash's upcoming mega project Toxic promises to shape the actor's global ambition. But, given the multiple delays, is he getting too obsessed about delivering the perfect global product with Toxic?

Jackie Shroff's The Great Grand Superhero gets a release date: What to expect Jackie Shroff-starrer The Great Grand Superhero finally has a release date. The fantasy drama explores childhood imagination, aliens and the bond between a grandfather and his grandson.

Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge to get extended raw version on OTT. Details Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge will stream overseas from May 15. It will feature extended raw footage that was not part of the theatrical version.

Shekhar Suman says his debut film Utsav was ahead of its time, wants re-release Shekhar Suman revisited the films that shaped his career and said Utsav was ahead of its time. He also explained why he has now launched a film academy with a modern, experience-led approach to acting.

Prime Video, MX Player integrate: What it means for India's OTT landscape Amazon is integrating Prime Video and MX Player into a unified streaming ecosystem in India. The move brings free and paid viewing under one umbrella while widening its advertising and audience reach.

Rohit Sharma set to make his TV debut, teaser goes viral Indian cricketer Rohit Sharma teased his television debut with a promo built around his viral on-field remark. The clip has sparked excitement among fans, while details of the show's format are still awaited.

Daldalll: Jaideep Ahlawat trapped in a swamp in first poster of his new film Jaideep Ahlawat unveiled the first-look of his upcoming film Daldalll. The actor is shown trapped in a swamp in the gritty poster.

Alia Bhatt reviews Daadi Ki Shaadi, says Neetu Kapoor, Riddhima light up the screen Alia Bhatt shared her reaction after watching Daadi Ki Shaadi and said the film left her with a "warm smile." The actor especially praised Neetu Kapoor and debutante Riddhima Kapoor Sahni, saying they lit up the screen with their performances.

Su From So director JP Thuminad marries longtime girlfriend Rashmika Actor-director JP Thuminad, known for Su From So, married his longtime partner Rashmika in a traditional ceremony in Mangaluru on Tuesday. Wishes poured in online for the couple.

Urvashi Rautela on receiving Valentine's Day wish from Balakrishna: So cute Urvashi Rautela recalled the time when Nandamuri Balakrishna was the first person to wish her on Valentine's Day, adding that she was lucky to work with him in Daaku Maharaaj.

general news.headline: Road clear for Vijay to be Tamil Nadu CM as VCK, IUML finally extend supportThe announcement by VCK and IUML ends four days of intense political negotiations after Vijay's TVK emerged as the single largest party in the Tamil Nadu elections.

Punjab minister Sanjeev Arora arrested by ED after raids; Kejriwal targets BJPThe ED conducted fresh searches at Punjab minister Sanjeev Arora's residence and linked premises in a new money laundering case. The action sharpened the AAP government's political clash with the BJP-led Centre ahead of the 2027 Assembly polls.

Not 'former CM', Mamata's new X bio sends political messageMamata Banerjee changed her X bio as Suvendu Adhikari took oath as West Bengal Chief Minister. The update carefully avoided calling her a former chief minister.

Live: Greatest transfer of power that's about to occur, says TVK after VCK's backingTamil Nadu Government Formation News Live Updates: Political tensions in Tamil Nadu intensified amid uncertainty over government formation. AMMK leader TTV Dhinakaran challenged TVK's claim of securing his party's support, demanding the original letter allegedly submitted to the Governor. He said AMMK MLA S Kamraj denied authorising such support and accused TVK of relying on a photocopied document, with a police complaint already filed. Kamraj further dismissed a video released by Vijay-led TVK as AI-generated. Meanwhile, VCK MP D Ravikumar rejected speculation that his party was bargaining for cabinet positions, accusing sections of the media of spreading misinformation. The volatile atmosphere deepened after a TVK supporter allegedly attempted self-immolation demanding Vijay become Chief Minister, prompting BJP leader Tamilisai Soundararajan to urge Vijay to calm supporters. Simultaneously, AIADMK MLAs met Edappadi K Palaniswami in Chennai, while Congress legislators remained camped in Hyderabad amid continuing political uncertainty. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Hantavirus explained: Early symptoms are mild, but organ failure follows fastAs global concern grows over the suspected hantavirus outbreak aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship, doctors are stressing that the infection behaves very differently from highly contagious respiratory viruses like Covid-19. Experts explain how the rodent-borne disease attacks the body, why the lungs and kidneys are most at risk, and why early medical care can be lifesaving.

Vijay hasn't just defeated DMK, he has made it friendless. Here's howVijay's TVK had defeated the ruling MK Stalin-led DMK in the Assembly election. Vijay didn't just weaken the DMK by snatching seats from it in direct contests, his path to the chief minister's office was made possible by the Dravida party's allies. The DMK is now left without its longtime friends.

Who is the 98-year-old BJP veteran whose feet PM Modi touched?Before the oath-taking ceremony in Kolkata, Prime Minister Narendra Modi touched the feet of 98-year-old Makhan Lal Sarkar, who was arrested in Kashmir in 1953 while accompanying Syama Prasad Mookerjee during the movement to hoist the Indian tricolour there.

Lieutenant General NS Raja Subramani named new Chief of Defence StaffLieutenant General NS Raja Subramani will replace General Anil Chauhan, who will be completing his tenure as CDS on May 30 this year. In another major military appointment, Vice Admiral Krishna Swaminathan has been appointed as the next Chief of the Indian Navy.

Mysterious rocket launch over Odisha: Is it India's new hypersonic missile?The Ministry of Defence confirmed that the mysterious object was an advanced Agni missile tested on May 8, 2026, one that can release multiple warheads mid-flight and strike several different targets at once.

Live: Congress high command meeting begins to pick CM, KC Venugopal leads raceKerala New CM Announcement Live Updates: The Congress high command is likely to take a call today on who will become the next Chief Minister of Kerala, where the UDF, led by the grand old party, stormed to a sweeping victory with 99 seats in the recently held Assembly polls. Congress leaders VD Satheesan, KC Venugopal and Ramesh Chennithala are being considered contenders for the top job in Kerala. Lobbying has intensified by the respective supporters of the three leaders. Satheesan and Chennithala arrived in Delhi on Friday amid the mounting anticipation over the key job. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates on the Kerala CM suspense.

Win for Tamil Nadu: Genelia D'Souza calls Vijay's election victory proud momentActor Genelia D'Souza said Thalapathy Vijay's election victory is a proud moment for Tamil Nadu. She said the result reflected public trust in him and faith that he would meet expectations.

Gujarat parents hired four bouncers for their teen daughter. Here's whyHandcrafted ByOur Editors Families in Ahmedabad and Surat reportedly brought in bouncers to monitor teenagers with severe phone and gaming dependence. Psychiatrists say the cases reflect deeper emotional distress and a wider digital dependency crisis among children.

A bird in hand, Bengal women chose not to understandWithout much to show the people during the West Bengal campaign, the Mamata Banerjee-led Trinamool Congress made its social welfare schemes, like Lakshmir Bhandar, its major poll plank. Going by the BJP landslide, women of West Bengal certainly didn't care much about the bird in hand.

Matua to Rajbanshi: BJP's Bengal Cabinet reflects social balancing actThe BJP's first government in West Bengal reflected a carefully balanced social and regional mix, with leaders from OBC, Kayastha, Matua, tribal and Rajbanshi communities finding representation in Suvendu Adhikari's cabinet.

Suvendu Adhikari's rise: From Nandigram giant killer to Bengal Chief MinisterOnce Mamata Banerjee's trusted lieutenant, Suvendu Adhikari is now the West Bengal Chief Minister after he took oath on Saturday after scripting the BJP's biggest breakthrough and defeating his former mentor once again.

Can motor insurers reject claims for reporting accidents late?Handcrafted ByOur Editors In the fifth story in our series Motor Mess, we spoke to experts to understand why delayed accident reporting often leads to insurance claim rejections, the mistakes policyholders should avoid and what options remain if such claims are rejected.

Can't focus anymore? Doctors warn reels may be rewiring your brainShort-form videos may feel entertaining and harmless, but experts warn that endless scrolling can slowly affect focus, sleep, and mental sharpness. From reduced attention spans to disturbed sleep cycles, the brain is paying a hidden price for constant digital stimulation.

Question Time: Weekly News QuizQuestion Time invites readers to test how closely they followed the week's news. The quiz turns the biggest developments into a quick check of current affairs awareness.

How Bengal erected BJP fence after Jamaat's rise along Bangladesh borderThe Jamaat-e-Islami saw a surge in Bangladesh in the February election, particularly along the border with West Bengal. The rise of the Islamist party seems to have resulted in voter consolidation for the BJP across the border. The West Bengal Assembly election results show that the BJP won several seats in pockets directly adjacent to the seats won by the Jamaat.

Yashasvi Jaiswal, Shafali Verma served notice for missing dope tests: SourcesYashasvi Jaiswal and Shafali Verma have received their first missed test notices from NADA after they were unavailable for scheduled dope tests. The cases fall under the whereabouts failure rule, where three lapses in 12 months can lead to a ban of up to two years.

Is Yash's global dream proving toxic to his pan-India superstardom?What began as anticipation has turned into a test of patience for fans. Yash's upcoming mega project Toxic promises to shape the actor's global ambition. But, given the multiple delays, is he getting too obsessed about delivering the perfect global product with Toxic?

OpenAI, Anthropic starting service companies is good news for Indian IT sectorAnthropic and OpenAI are entering the world of SaaS – software as a service – but with AI. This may come across as bad news for Indian IT giants like TCS and Infosys. But look closer, it is actually very good news for them and for Indian IT employees.

Who is IPS B Sumathi who went undercover at night to test safety and faced 40 men?From a small Telangana village to the streets of Hyderabad at midnight, IPS officer B Sumathi's journey is as fearless as it is inspiring. Her undercover operation to test women's safety has sparked conversations across the country and offered a reality check on what women face daily.

Live: Mamata Banerjee urges opposition parties to unite against BJPSuvendu Adhikari Oath Ceremony Live Updates: BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari took oath as the first BJP chief minister of West Bengal at a grand ceremony at Kolkata's Brigade Parade Grounds on Saturday in the presence of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Union Home Minister Amit Shah, several Union ministers and chief ministers from NDA-ruled states. Senior BJP leaders including Dilip Ghosh, Agnimitra Paul, Nisith Pramanik and Kshudiram Tudu also took oath as ministers in the new government.

Vijay Deverakonda stuns in Rowdy Janardhana poster as Keerthy Suresh takes chargeThe makers of Rowdy Janardhana unveiled a birthday poster showing Vijay Deverakonda and Keerthy Suresh in a striking face-off. The poster teases the film's mix of rural action and romance ahead of its December 2026 release.

Nora Fatehi to perform at FIFA World Cup 2026 opening ceremony in CanadaNora Fatehi is set to perform at the FIFA World Cup 2026 opening ceremony in Canada on June 12. The announcement marks her return to the FIFA stage as the tournament opens across three host countries.

sports.headline: Sri Lanka Cricket drops social media rule for Lanka Premier League after backlash Sri Lanka Cricket has removed the 250,000-follower threshold for Lanka Premier League Icon and Star players after criticism. The retreat fixes one embarrassment, but the tournament still leans heavily on social media promotion.

Stubbs is overrated: Ex-IND selector tears into DC star as poor run continues Kris Srikkanth tore into the Delhi Capitals after their batting crumbled against KKR, singling out Tristan Stubbs. Srikkanth said that the Delhi middle-order is finished after they slipped to a loss to KKR.

Chess GM Abhijeet Gupta exposes Odisha chess tournament for unpaid prize money Abhijeet Gupta has accused the Odisha Open organisers of withholding his prize money despite repeated assurances. Abhijeet approached the sports ministry to get involved in the matter to resolve the issue.

Fan's phone turns off after Rohit Sharma agrees to selfie, what's next is hilarious Rohit Sharma found himself in an awkward fan interaction before Mumbai Indians' match, but the veteran batter's focus will remain on carrying his strong comeback form into the crucial stage of the season.

Wrestling Federation bars Vinesh Phogat from competing in comeback tournament The Wrestling Federation of India has barred Vinesh Phogat from the National Open Ranking Tournament in Gonda. The decision halts her planned return after reversing her post-Paris Olympics retirement.

Asian Beach Games | Shifting sands of Sanya: Where grit is all you have At the Asian Beach Games in Sanya, the sand turned every event into a contest of endurance, skill and nerve. The medals mattered, but the deeper story was how athletes from India and across Asia met hardship head-on.

Stuttering Rajasthan Royals take on resurgent Gujarat Titans in Jaipur encounter RR vs GT: Rajasthan and Gujarat meet in Jaipur with play-off places up for grabs. Two contrasting teams, one critical match. Who blinks first in the top-four race?

Not the next IPL: India Basketball League wants its own court, own culture India's first professional basketball league is not trying to become the next IPL, and that may be its smartest move. The IBL wants to build a sustainable ecosystem, giving young players a genuine career pathway instead of chasing cricket's blueprint.

Chahal should be behind bars: Ex-spinner on PBKS star after alleged vaping row Laxman Sivaramakrishnan has demanded action after a viral clip allegedly showed Yuzvendra Chahal vaping on a Punjab Kings flight. The former spinner said that Chahal should be behind bars for his actions.

Spinning a web: How KKR spinners have bounced back to reignite team's fading hopes IPL 2026, DC vs KKR: Kolkata Knight Riders beat the Delhi Capitals by eight wickets after their spin trio of Sunil Narine, Varun Chakravarthy and Anukul Roy put their best foot forward at the Arun Jaitley Stadium.

Yashasvi Jaiswal, Shafali Verma served notice for missing dope tests: Sources Yashasvi Jaiswal and Shafali Verma have received their first missed test notices from NADA after they were unavailable for scheduled dope tests. The cases fall under the whereabouts failure rule, where three lapses in 12 months can lead to a ban of up to two years.

F1 bows to Verstappen, drivers criticism, agrees to massive 2027 engine shake-upFormula 1 and the teams have agreed in principle to alter the 2027 engine rules after drivers objected to excessive battery dependence. The move comes after the governing body decided to heed the demands from the drivers after criticism early on.

Djokovic reveals worrying fitness truth after shock first-round exit in RomeNovak Djokovic has admitted recurring physical issues are now part of his reality after a shock Italian Open exit, raising fresh concerns over his fitness ahead of the French Open.

DC vs KKR: How Green avoided Kolkata's most hated man tag for Finn Allen hundredFinn Allen blasted a 47-ball hundred against Delhi Capitals as Cameron Green carefully played second fiddle to allow his partner to get to the landmark. Green said he did the maths to avoid denying Allen his maiden ton, a stand that sealed KKR's fourth straight win.

New RR owner Laxmi Mittal meets team ahead of GT tie, chats with Riyan, JaiswalNew Rajasthan Royals owner Lakshmi Mittal visited the squad in Jaipur and spoke at length with Riyan Parag and Yashasvi Jaiswal. The appearance came as the franchise chases a play-off place while a multibillion-dollar ownership change moves towards completion.

IPL Play of the Day: KKR's spin barrage turns Delhi's route away from playoffsIPL 2026, DC vs KKR: Kolkata Knight Riders' spinners choked Delhi Capitals through the middle overs after the home team got off to a brisk start at Arun Jaitley Stadium. On a retro night in Delhi, Sunil Narine, Anukul Roy and Varun Chakravarthy brought in the disco twist to keep KKR alive in the play-off race.

How KKR can qualify for playoffs after incredible comeback: Qualification ScenarioIPL 2026, DC vs KKR: Ajinkya Rahane's Kolkata Knight Riders have more than a realistic chance to qualify for the playoffs after they beat the Delhi Capitals by 8 wickets on Friday in New Delhi.

The Jason Holder effect: Gujarat Titans enjoying all-rounder's IPL 2026 surgeIPL 2026: Parthiv Patel said Jason Holder's inclusion has changed Gujarat Titans' balance ahead of the Rajasthan Royals match. His all-round returns have stabilised the middle order and strengthened the bowling options.

No Instagram following, no LPL: Sri Lanka League sets odd criteria for playersLanka Premier League has set odd criteria for categorising its players. If a player wants to be in the league as a top tier Icon category, they need to either play 75 T20Is or have, wait for it, 250,000 social media followers.

Novak Djokovic knocked out of Italian Open by his mirror imageNovak Djokovic suffered a shock early exit at the Italian Open after Croatian youngster Dino Prizmic fought back from a set down to beat the Serbian 2-6, 6-2, 6-4 in Rome. The defeat has raised fresh concerns over his fitness and form ahead of the French Open later this month.

No deal between India and Russia in athletics: AFI snubs rumoursThe Athletics Federation of India has denied reports claiming that it signed a cooperation agreement with Russian Athletics to allow Russian athletes to compete in India. AFI spokesperson Adille Sumariwalla said India would continue to follow World Athletics sanctions imposed on Russia.

Finn-ishing blow: KKR back in playoffs race as DC's home misery continuesIPL 2026, DC vs KKR: A Finn Allen century and a spin-bowling masterclass saw Kolkata Knight Riders crush Delhi Capitals at the Kotla. The clinical win revives KKR's playoff hopes while leaving Delhi's campaign in tatters.

Season finished: Delhi Capitals to start planning for next year, says Axar PatelDelhi Capitals captain Axar Patel admitted that his side has effectively fallen out of the IPL 2026 playoffs race after their defeat to Kolkata Knight Riders. Axar said Delhi will now start planning for next season and test their bench strength.

IPL 2026: Angkrish Raghuvanshi's streak of bizarre dismissals continuesIPL 2026: Angkrish Raghuvanshi suffered another bizarre dismissal during Kolkata Knight Riders' chase against Delhi Capitals. The freak wicket added to a season already marked by unusual exits, though this one brought laughter rather than controversy.

Stop treating CWG, Asian Games like paid holidays: Govt warns administratorsIndian sports administrators have been warned to not treat events like Commonwealth and Asian Games like paid vacations. Sources say that if found lackadaisical, they will be sent back home midway through the tournament.

Madrid fine Valverde, Tchouameni Rs 5.5 core each after mega brawl in trainingReal Madrid have fined Federico Valverde and Aurelien Tchouameni Rs 5.5 crore each after the pair were involved in a heated training-ground altercation ahead of El Clasico. The club confirmed that both players apologised to each other, the squad and the fans before accepting the punishment.

Gautam Gambhir seen at DC vs KKR, shares long chat with India selectorIPL 2026: India head coach Gautam Gambhir attended the Delhi Capitals-Kolkata Knight Riders match and spent long periods with selector R P Singh. Their presence together at Arun Jaitley Stadium stirred talk of scouting and selection ahead of a busy international schedule.

Bournemouth player dropped after club probes alleged chats with minorAFC Bournemouth have left Alex Jimenez out of their squad after online posts prompted an internal investigation. The development comes at a key stage in the club's push for European qualification.

Aryna Sabalenka to remain neutral athlete as ITF rejects IOC's Belarus policy stanceThe ITF has confirmed that Russian and Belarusian players, including Aryna Sabalenka, will continue competing as neutral athletes despite the IOC's recommendation to ease restrictions on Belarusian competitors.

Hopeful Guardiola wants Manchester City to keep pressure on Arsenal in title raceManchester City manager Pep Guardiola has urged his side to keep the pressure firmly on Arsenal in the Premier League title race, insisting City can only focus on winning their remaining matches as the season heads into its decisive stretch.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
